enriched_requirements.full_sentence:

If such schemes are announced frequently, incommensurately, or without due

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks
- Resolution of Stressed Assets) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: Bank of India (Small Finance Banks - Resolution of Stressed Assets) Directions, 2025

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall come into force with immediate effect unless specified
otherwise.
enriched_requirements.object: immediate effect unless specified otherwise

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be applicable to Small Finance Banks (hereinafter
collectively referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank').
enriched_requirements.object: Finance Banks (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank')

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The instructions contained in Chapter III and Chapter IV shall not be applicable
to revival and rehabilitation of MSMEs covered by the instructions contained in
Circular No.
enriched_requirements.object: and rehabilitation of MSMEs covered by the instructions contained in Circular No

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall not be applicable for borrower entities in respect of which
specific directions have already been issued or are issued by the Reserve Bank

Bankruptcy Code, 2016.

A bank shall pursue such cases as per the specific
instructions issued to them.
enriched_requirements.object: per the specific instructions issued to them

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In these Directions, the following definitions shall apply, unless the context
otherwise requires:
(1) 'aggregate exposure' shall include all fund based and non-fund based
exposure, including investment exposure;

amount due from the borrower on the part of the bank with corresponding

A bank shall put in place Board-approved policies for resolution of stressed
assets, including the timelines for resolution as well as detailed policies on

A non-exhaustive indicative list of signs of financial difficulty, which is based on

for reference:
(1) A default, as per the definition provided in the framework, shall be treated
as an indicator for financial difficulty, irrespective of reasons for the
default.

A bank shall complement the above list of non-exhaustive enumeration of
financial difficulty indicators with key financial ratios and operational parameters
which may include quantitative and qualitative aspects.

(1) comprehensive prescription of the process to be followed for all
compromise settlements and technical write-offs, with specific guidance

In cases where resolution plan is to be implemented, all specified lenders, shall

A bank shall make additional specific provision of 15 per cent for an account
referred to in paragraphs 61 and 62 at the end of the Review Period.
enriched_requirements.object: of 15 per cent for an account referred to in paragraphs 61 and 62 at the end of the Review Period

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Specific provisions held on restructured assets by a bank as per paragraph 64
above, may be reversed when the accounts are upgraded to standard category.
enriched_requirements.object: accounts are upgraded to standard category

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Additional specific provisions as required under paragraph 64, along with other
additional provisions, may be reversed as per the norms laid down in paragraph
50 by a bank.
enriched_requirements.object: the norms laid down in paragraph 50 by a bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
During the insolvency resolution process period, provisioning for the interim
finance shall be governed by Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks -
Income Recognition, Asset Classification and Provisioning) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: Bank of India (Small Finance Banks - Income Recognition, Asset Classification and Provisioning) Directions, 2025

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Subsequently, upon approval of the resolution plan by the Adjudicating Authority,
treatment of such interim finance shall be as per the norms applicable to
additional finance, as per paragraphs 55 and 56
enriched_requirements.object: norms applicable to additional finance, as per paragraphs 55 and 56

conditions: where a final resolution plan
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In respect of accounts of borrowers where a final resolution plan, as approved
by the Committee of Creditors, has been submitted by the Resolution
Professional for approval of the Adjudicating Authority (in terms of section 30(6)
of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016), a bank may keep the provisions
held as on the date of such submission of RP frozen for a period of six months
from the date of submission of the plan or up to ninety days from the date of
approval of the resolution plan by the Adjudicating Authority in terms of section
31 (1) of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016, whichever is earlier.
enriched_requirements.object: as on the date of such submission of RP frozen for a period of six months from the date of submission of the plan or up to ni

conditions:
where the provisioning held is lower than the expected
required provisioning
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that in cases where the provisioning held is lower than the expected
required provisioning, a bank shall make additional provisioning to the extent of
the shortfall.
enriched_requirements.object: the extent of the shortfall

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The facility of freezing the quantum of the provision as per paragraph 70 shall be
available only in cases where the provisioning held by a bank as on the date of
submission of the plan for approval of the Adjudicating Authority is more than the
expected provisioning required to be held in the normal course upon
implementation of the approved resolution plan, taking into account the contours
of the resolution plan approved by Committee of Creditors / Adjudicating
Authority, as the case may be, and extant prudential norms.
enriched_requirements.object: cases where the provisioning held by a bank as on the date of submission of the plan for approval of the Adjudicating Authority i

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding paragraph 70, a bank shall not reverse the excess provisions
held as on the date of submission of the resolution plan for approval of the
Adjudicating Authority at this stage.
enriched_requirements.object: held as on the date of submission of the resolution plan for approval of the Adjudicating Authority at this stage

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Subsequent to the lapse of the period specified in paragraph 70, provisioning
shall be as per the requirements in the Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance
<image>
Banks - Income Recognition, Asset Classification and Provisioning) Directions,
2025.
enriched_requirements.object: requirements in the Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance <image> Banks - Income Recognition, Asset Classification and Provisioning) Dire

conditions:
if the Adjudicating Authority rejects the resolution plan thus
submitted
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The facility of freezing of provisions as allowed in paragraph 70 shall lapse
immediately if the Adjudicating Authority rejects the resolution plan thus
submitted, and asset classification in respect of such borrower shall continue be
governed by the extant asset classification norms.
enriched_requirements.object: Adjudicating Authority rejects the resolution plan thus submitted, and asset classification in respect of such borrower shall

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Interest income in respect of restructured accounts classified as 'standard
assets' may be recognized on accrual basis and that in respect of the
restructured accounts classified as 'non-performing assets' shall be recognised
on cash basis.
enriched_requirements.object: basis

conditions:
where the pre-restructuring facilities
were classified as non-performing assets
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In the case of additional finance in accounts where the pre-restructuring facilities
were classified as non-performing assets, the interest income shall be
recognised only on cash basis by a bank except when the restructuring is
accompanied by a change in ownership.
enriched_requirements.object: cash basis by a bank except when the restructuring is accompanied by a change in ownership

conditions: In case of change in ownership of the borrowing entities
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In case of change in ownership of the borrowing entities, credit facilities of the
concerned borrowing entities may be continued / upgraded as 'standard' by a
bank after the change in ownership is implemented, either under the Insolvency
and Bankruptcy Code, 2016 or under these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: as 'standard' by a bank after the change in ownership is implemented, either under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016 or

conditions: If the change in ownership is implemented under these Directions
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If the change in ownership is implemented under these Directions, then the
classification as 'standard' shall be subject to the following conditions:
(1) The bank shall conduct necessary due diligence in this regard and
clearly establish that the acquirer is not a person disqualified in terms of
Section 29A of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016.
enriched_requirements.object: following conditions: (1) The bank shall conduct necessary due diligence in this regard and clearly establish that the acquirer is n

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The bank shall clearly establish that the 'new promoter' is not a person /
entity / subsidiary / associate etc.
enriched_requirements.object: 'new promoter' is not a person / entity / subsidiary / associate etc

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The new promoter shall have acquired at least 26 per cent of the paid-
up equity capital as well as voting rights of the borrower entity and shall
be the single largest shareholder of the borrower entity.
enriched_requirements.object: 26 per cent of the paid- up equity capital as well as voting rights of the borrower entity and shall be the single largest shar

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(4) The new promoter shall be in 'control' of the borrower entity as per the
definition of 'control' in the Companies Act, 2013 / regulations issued by
the Securities and Exchange Board of India / any other applicable
regulations / accounting standards, as the case may be.
enriched_requirements.object: the borrower entity as per the definition of 'control' in the Companies Act, 2013 / regulations issued by the Securities and Exchan

conditions:
If the account fails to perform satisfactorily at any point of time during the
monitoring period
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If the account fails to perform satisfactorily at any point of time during the
monitoring period, it shall trigger a fresh Review Period.
enriched_requirements.object: Period

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The quantum of provisions held (excluding additional provisions) by the bank
against the said account as on the date of change in ownership of the borrowing
entities may be reversed only after the end of monitoring period subject to
satisfactory performance during the same.
enriched_requirements.object: the end of monitoring period subject to satisfactory performance during the same

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In case the promoters' shares have been pledged with a bank as collateral during
the lock-in period mandated under ecurities and Exchange Board of India (Issue
of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) (ICDR) Regulations, 2018, and the
pledge is invoked during the lock-in period by the bank consequent to default by
the company, such shares may be transferred to the bank but shall continue to
be under lock-in in the hands of the bank for the remaining lock-in period.
enriched_requirements.object: lock-in in the hands of the bank for the remaining lock-in period

conditions:
if all the
following conditions are met:
(1) the seller of the assets is in financial difficulty
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A sale and leaseback transaction of the assets of a borrower or other
transactions of similar nature shall be treated as an event of restructuring for the
purpose of asset classification and provisioning in the books of a bank with
regard to the residual debt of the seller as well as the debt of the buyer if all the
following conditions are met:
(1) the seller of the assets is in financial difficulty;
enriched_requirements.object: event of restructuring for the purpose of asset classification and provisioning in the books of a bank with regard to the residual de

conditions:
where permitted) from the Indian banking system in the
form of Guarantees / Standby Letters of Credit / Letters of Comfort
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
or
(2) with the support (where permitted) from the Indian banking system in the
form of Guarantees / Standby Letters of Credit / Letters of Comfort, etc.,
such events shall be treated as 'restructuring' by a bank if the borrower
concerned is under financial difficulty.
enriched_requirements.object: by a bank if the borrower concerned is under financial difficulty

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Borrowers who have committed frauds/ malfeasance/ wilful default as well as
any entity with which a wilful defaulter is associated shall remain ineligible for
restructuring.

However, in cases where the existing promoters are replaced by new promoters
satisfying the conditions specified at sub-paragraphs (1) to (4) of paragraph 77,
and the borrower company is totally delinked from such erstwhile promoters /
management, a bank may take a view on restructuring such accounts based on
their viability, without prejudice to the continuance of criminal action against the
erstwhile promoters / management.
enriched_requirements.object: restructuring such accounts based on their viability, without prejudice to the continuance of criminal action against the erstwhile pro

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A wilful defaulter or any entity with which a wilful defaulter is associated shall be
eligible for restructuring subsequent to removal of the name of wilful defaulter
from the List of Wilful Defaulters, subject to penal measures applicable to
borrowers classified as wilful defaulter in terms of the Reserve Bank of India
(Small Finance Banks - Treatment of Wilful Defaulters and Large Defaulters)
Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: subsequent to removal of the name of wilful defaulter from the List of Wilful Defaulters

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The objective of compromise settlements shall be to maximise the possible
recovery from a distressed borrower at minimum expense, in the best interest of
the bank.
enriched_requirements.object: possible recovery from a distressed borrower at minimum expense, in the best interest of the bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The compromise settlements and technical write-offs shall be without prejudice
to any mutually agreed contractual provisions between a bank and a borrower
relating to future contingent realizations or recovery by the bank, subject to such
claims not being recognised in any manner on the balance sheet of the bank at
the time of the settlement or subsequently till actual realization of such
receivables.
enriched_requirements.object: any mutually agreed contractual provisions between a bank and a borrower relating to future contingent realizations or recovery by the bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
Provided that any such claims recognised on the balance sheet of the bank shall
render the arrangement to be treated as restructuring.
enriched_requirements.object: be treated as restructuring

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding paragraph 89, compromise settlements where the time for
payment of the agreed settlement amount exceeds three months shall be treated
as restructuring.

Any arrangement involving part settlement with the borrower shall also fall under
the definition of restructuring, and shall be governed by the provisions applicable
thereto.
enriched_requirements.object: definition of restructuring, and shall be governed by the provisions applicable thereto

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
However, such technical write-offs do not entail any waiver of claims against the
borrower and thus the bank's right to recovery shall not be undermined in any
manner.
enriched_requirements.object: manner

conditions: In case of partial technical write-offs
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In case of partial technical write-offs, the prudential requirements in respect of
residual exposure, including provisioning and asset classification, shall be with
reference to the original exposure.
enriched_requirements.object: the original exposure

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that the amount of provision including the amount representing partial
technical write-off shall meet the extant provisioning requirements, as computed
on the gross value of the asset.
enriched_requirements.object: requirements, as computed on the gross value of the asset

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
There shall be a reporting mechanism to the next higher authority, at least on a
quarterly basis, with respect to compromise settlements and technical write offs
approved by a particular authority.
enriched_requirements.object: to the next higher authority, at least on a quarterly basis, with respect to compromise settlements and technical write offs

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that compromise settlements and technical write-offs approved by the
MD & CEO / Board Level Committee shall be reported to the Board.
enriched_requirements.object: Board

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall mandate a suitable reporting format so as to ensure adequate
coverage of the following aspects at the minimum:
(1) trend in number of accounts and amounts subjected to compromise
settlement and/or technical write-off (q-o-q and y-o-y);
enriched_requirements.object: format so as to ensure adequate coverage of the following aspects at the minimum: (1) trend in number of accounts and amo

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In respect of borrowers subject to compromise settlements, there shall be a
cooling period as determined by the respective Board approved policies before
the bank can assume fresh exposures to such borrowers.
enriched_requirements.object: as determined by the respective Board approved policies before the bank can assume fresh exposures to such borrowers

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that the cooling period in respect of exposures other than farm credit
exposures shall be subject to a floor of 12 months with a bank being free to
stipulate higher cooling periods in terms of their Board approved policies.
enriched_requirements.object: floor of 12 months with a bank being free to stipulate higher cooling periods in terms of their Board approved policies

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provided further that the cooling period for farm credit exposures shall be
determined by a bank as per their respective Board approved policies.
enriched_requirements.object: bank as per their respective Board approved policies

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: Farm credit for the above purpose shall refer to credit extended to
agricultural activities as listed in the Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance
Banks - Income Recognition, Asset Classification and Provisioning) Directions,
2025.
enriched_requirements.object: to agricultural activities as listed in the Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks - Income Recognition, Asset Classifica

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The cooling period to be adopted in respect of exposures subjected to technical
write-offs shall be as per the Board approved policies of a bank.
enriched_requirements.object: Board approved policies of a bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank may undertake compromise settlements or technical write-offs in respect
of accounts categorised as wilful defaulters or fraud without prejudice to the
criminal proceeding underway against such borrowers.
enriched_requirements.object: technical write-offs in respect of accounts categorised as wilful defaulters or fraud without prejudice to the cri

conditions:
where a bank enter into
compromise settlement with such borrowers
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The penal measures applicable to borrowers classified as fraud or wilful defaulter
in terms of the Reserve Bank of India (Fraud Risk Management in Commercial
Banks (including Regional Rural Banks) and All India Financial Institutions)
Directions, 2024 and the Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks -
Treatment of Wilful Defaulters and Large Defaulters) Directions, 2025,
respectively, shall continue to be applicable in cases where a bank enter into
compromise settlement with such borrowers, and the cooling periods specified
in paragraphs 96 and 97, in respect of such borrowers, shall be without prejudice
to such penal measures.
enriched_requirements.object: in cases where a bank enter into compromise settlement with such borrowers, and the cooling periods specified in paragraphs

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
As long as larger policy concerns are
suitably addressed and the costs of malafide actions are made to be borne by
the perpetrators, early recoveries by a bank should be a preferred option,
subject to safeguards.

The compromise settlements with the borrowers under these Directions shall be
without prejudice to the provisions of any other statute in force.
enriched_requirements.object: the provisions of any other statute in force

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Wherever a bank had commenced recovery proceedings under a judicial forum
and the same is pending before such judicial forum, any settlement arrived at
with the borrower shall be subject to obtaining a consent decree from the
concerned judicial authorities.
enriched_requirements.object: a consent decree from the concerned judicial authorities

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In addition to the requirements contained in paragraphs 87 to 101, the following
shall apply in cases of compromise settlements undertaken by a bank through Lok
Adalats:
(1) The monetary ceiling of the cases to be referred to the Lok Adalats,
organised by Civil Courts shall be ₹20 lakh.
enriched_requirements.object: compromise settlements undertaken by a bank through Lok Adalats: (1) The monetary ceiling of the cases to be referred to the Lok Ada

conditions: where financial closure has been achieved as on October 1
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The instructions contained in this paragraphs 106 to 121 shall not apply to
projects where financial closure has been achieved as on October 1, 2025, for
which the prudential guidelines on project finance prevailing before October 1,
2025, which otherwise shall be treated as repealed, shall apply.
enriched_requirements.object: financial closure has been achieved as on October 1, 2025, for which the prudential guidelines on project finance prevailing b

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding the instructions in paragraph 104, any resolution of a fresh credit
event and/or change in material terms and conditions in the loan contract in such
projects, on or after October 1, 2025, shall be as per the guidelines contained in
paragraphs 106 to 124.
enriched_requirements.object: guidelines contained in paragraphs 106 to 124

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall monitor the performance of the project and any buildup of stress on
an ongoing basis and shall be expected to initiate a resolution plan well in
advance.
enriched_requirements.object: the project and any buildup of stress on an ongoing basis and shall be expected to initiate a resolution plan well in advan

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Occurrence of a credit event with any lender during the construction phase, shall
trigger a collective resolution in terms of Chapter III of these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: in terms of Chapter III of these Directions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: The reference to 'default' in Chapter III of these Directions shall be
read as 'credit event' for the purpose of project finance accounts, unless
specified otherwise.
enriched_requirements.object: event' for the purpose of project finance accounts

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Any such credit event with a bank shall be reported to the Central Repository of
Information on Large Credit (CRILC) by the bank in the prescribed weekly as
well as the CRILC-Main report in compliance with paragraphs 17and 18
enriched_requirements.object: Central Repository of Information on Large Credit (CRILC) by the bank in the prescribed weekly as well as the CRILC-Main report in

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank, which is part of a consortium / multiple lending arrangement shall also
report occurrence of such credit event to all other members of the consortium /
multiple lending arrangement.
enriched_requirements.object: such credit event to all other members of the consortium / multiple lending arrangement

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The instructions on CRILC reporting as per paragraph 108 shall be issued in due
course.
enriched_requirements.object: course

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall undertake a prima facie review of the borrower account during the
Review Period.
enriched_requirements.object: review of the borrower account during the Review Period

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The conduct of a bank during the Review Period, including signing of Inter
Creditor Agreement (ICA), and the decision to implement a resolution plan,
wherever required, shall be guided by Chapter III of these Directions, unless
specified otherwise.
enriched_requirements.object: III of these Directions

conditions: If a resolution plan involving extension of original / extended DCCO
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If a resolution plan involving extension of original / extended DCCO, as the case
maybe, is implemented in a project finance account, which is classified as
Standard and satisfies all relevant prudential conditions specified for sanction,
disbursement and monitoring of project finance in the Reserve Bank of India
(Small Finance Banks - Credit Facilities) Directions, 2025, the asset
classification of such account shall continue to be classified as 'Standard',
provided the envisaged resolution plan ab initio conforms to the following
conditions:
(1) Permitted DCCO Deferment - Original / extended DCCO, as the case
may be, is extended, along with the consequential shift in repayment
enriched_requirements.object: as 'Standard'

conditions:
where aggregate exposure of all lenders
is less than ₹100 crore
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Cost Overrun - A bank may finance, as part of a resolution plan, cost
overrun associated with permitted DCCO deferment in compliance with
Explanation: For projects where aggregate exposure of all lenders
is less than ₹100 crore, internal credit rating may be considered,
if the project was originally not credit rated externally.
enriched_requirements.object: a resolution plan, cost overrun associated with permitted DCCO deferment in compliance with Explanation: For projects where aggregate exposure of all lenders is less than ₹100 crore, internal credit rating may be considered

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Change in Scope and Size - A project finance account where DCCO
extension is necessitated by an increase in the project outlay on account
of increase in scope and size of the project, may be classified as
'Standard', subject to complying with the following conditions:

The standard asset classification benefit on account of 'change in scope' shall
be allowed only once during the lifetime of the project.
enriched_requirements.object: during the lifetime of the project

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In all the cases specified in paragraph 113, the following conditions shall be
required to be met in respect of all the lenders before the expiry of 180 days from
the end of the Review Period, for successful implementation of a resolution plan:
(1) all required documentation, including execution of necessary
agreements between a lender and the borrower / creation of security
charge / perfection of securities, are completed in consonance with the
resolution plan being implemented;
enriched_requirements.object: met in respect of all the lenders before the expiry of 180 days from the end of the Review Period, for successful implementation of

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If a resolution plan involving change in DCCO is not successfully implemented
in terms of paragraphs 113 and / 115then the account shall be downgraded to
NPA immediately.
enriched_requirements.object: immediately

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: Satisfactory performance as defined in paragraph 6(14) shall apply
in this case.

A project finance account downgraded to NPA for non-compliance with
paragraph 115, may be upgraded on successful implementation of resolution
plan, provided no further request for deferment of DCCO is received.
enriched_requirements.object: implementation of resolution plan

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Income recognition in respect of a project finance account where a resolution
plans Involving Extension of Original / Extended DCCO shall be as follows:
(1) A bank may recognise income on accrual basis in respect of project
finance exposures which are classified as 'standard'.
enriched_requirements.object: A bank may recognise income on accrual basis in respect of project finance exposures which are classified as 'standard'

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) For NPAs, income recognition shall be as per instructions contained in
the Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks - Income Recognition,
Asset Classification and Provisioning) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: contained in the Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks - Income Recognition, Asset Classification and Provisioning) Direct

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For accounts which have availed DCCO deferment as per paragraphs 113 to
116 and are classified as 'standard', a bank shall maintain additional specific
provisions of 0.375% for infrastructure project loans and 0.5625% for non-
infrastructure project loans (including CRE and CRE-RH), for each quarter of
deferment, over and above the applicable standard asset provision specified in
the Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks - Income Recognition, Asset
Classification and Provisioning) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: of 0

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The additional specific provisions required as per paragraph 120 shall be
reversed upon commencement of commercial operations.
enriched_requirements.object: of commercial operations

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions stipulated in paragraph 120 shall not be applicable for existing
projects which are specified in paragraph 104 and such project loans shall
continue to be guided by the prudential guidelines for the purpose of provisioning,
prevailing before October 1, 2025 shall apply, which otherwise shall be treated
as repealed.
enriched_requirements.object: projects which are specified in paragraph 104 and such project loans shall continue to be guided by the prudential guidelin

conditions: if these were sanctioned on or after October 1
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding the instructions in paragraph 122, in case of any resolution of a
fresh credit event and / or change in material terms and conditions in the loan
contract in such projects, the provisions stipulated in paragraph 120 shall apply
to these projects as if these were sanctioned on or after October 1, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: as if these were sanctioned on or after October 1, 2025

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Provisioning for project loans classified as 'standard' (construction or operational
phase) or NPA shall be as per extant instructions contained in the Reserve Bank
of India (Small Finance Banks - Income Recognition, Asset Classification and
Provisioning) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: instructions contained in the Reserve Bank of India (Small Finance Banks - Income Recognition, Asset Classification and Provisioning)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The instructions contained in this Chapter shall be applicable to resolution of
exposures of borrowers impacted by a natural calamity or, mutatis mutandis,
exposures of borrowers impacted by external events such as riots / disturbances
that result in loss to economic activity (hereinafter collectively referred to as
'calamity'), upon the declaration of such calamity by the Central / State Governments
(in accordance with the framework placed by the concerned Government for this
purpose).
enriched_requirements.object: of exposures of borrowers impacted by a natural calamity or, mutatis mutandis, exposures of borrowers impacted by external

conditions:
where relief measures have
been already provided as on the effective date of these Amendment Directions
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These instructions shall not apply to borrower accounts where relief measures have
been already provided as on the effective date of these Amendment Directions, and
such accounts shall continue to be guided by the existing prudential guidelines.
enriched_requirements.object: where relief measures have been already provided as on the effective date of these Amendment Directions, and such accounts

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
However, any fresh resolution in such accounts under this Chapter, subsequent to
the effective date, shall be as per the guidelines contained in this Chapter.
enriched_requirements.object: guidelines contained in this Chapter

conditions:
In case no specific date of occurrence is ascertainable in respect of a
calamity
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: In case no specific date of occurrence is ascertainable in respect of a
calamity, the date of declaration of such calamity by the Central / State
Governments shall be treated as the date of occurrence.
enriched_requirements.object: date of occurrence

conditions:
if a larger part of the State / Union Territory (UT)
has been affected
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Upon declaration of a calamity, if a larger part of the State / Union Territory (UT)
has been affected, the SLBC / UTLBC convenor bank shall convene a special SLBC
/ UTLBC meeting within 15 days of such declaration.
enriched_requirements.object: / UTLBC meeting within 15 days of such declaration

conditions: If the calamity has affected only a part of the State / UT
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If the calamity has affected only a part of the State / UT, the convenor of the DCC
of the affected district (s) shall convene the meeting within 15 days of such
declaration, after due consultation with the SLBC / UTLBC convenor bank.
enriched_requirements.object: 15 days of such declaration, after due consultation with the SLBC / UTLBC convenor bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In the special SLBC / UTLBC / DCC meeting, the position of the affected areas may
be assessed in terms of the severity of the impact of the calamity on the economic
activity and the need for any resolution measures by the REs.
enriched_requirements.object: of the severity of the impact of the calamity on the economic activity and the need for any resolution measures by the REs

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
SLBC / UTLBC / DCC
may also formulate the objective criteria for identifying impacted borrowers, and the
extent of moratorium period required, if any.
enriched_requirements.object: criteria for identifying impacted borrowers, and the extent of moratorium period required

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The decisions taken in the special SLBC / UTLBC meeting(s) as mentioned at
paragraph 124E above, shall be conveyed by the SLBC convenor bank immediately
to all SLBC / UTLBC members along with the minutes of the meeting.
enriched_requirements.object: SLBC convenor bank immediately to all SLBC / UTLBC members along with the minutes of the meeting

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A copy of the
same shall also be forwarded by the SLBC / UTLBC convenor bank to the respective
Regional Office of Reserve Bank and the Non-Banking Financial Companies
(NBFCs) / Urban Co-operative Banks (UCBs) operating in the area.
enriched_requirements.object: the SLBC / UTLBC convenor bank to the respective Regional Office of Reserve Bank and the Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:

In cases where the funds received by a bank as part of the scheme are not

Provided that any changes / modifications to the terms and conditions of the

enriched_requirements.object: commercial discretion of the bank under relevant internal policy

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:

enriched_requirements.object: extant guidelines in this regard

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:

For the purpose of the Model Operating Procedure (MOP), Debt Relief Schemes (DRS)
refer to Schemes notified by the State Governments that entail funding by the fiscal
authorities to cover debt obligations of a targeted segment of borrowers that the regulated
entities are required to sacrifice / waive.

Announcement / notification of any such DRS should include the specific stress or
distress situation necessitating announcement of such support.
enriched_requirements.object: or distress situation necessitating announcement of such support

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Given the broader
implications of such DRS for the credit culture, while broad based relief measures can be
addressed through pure fiscal support in the form of Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT), DRS
should be considered only as a measure of last resort when other measures to alleviate
financial stress have failed.
enriched_requirements.object: a measure of last resort when other measures to alleviate financial stress have failed

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Before announcing any DRS, Governments may engage with the State Level Bankers'
Committee (SLBC)/ District level Consultative Committee (DCC) to evolve a coordinated
action plan for conceptualisation, design, and implementation of the DRS.
enriched_requirements.object: Level Bankers' Committee (SLBC)/ District level Consultative Committee (DCC) to evolve a coordinated action plan for conceptuali

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The schemes
should, cover critical aspects of the scheme like identification of borrowers, impact
assessment, implementation timelines, resolution of issues concerning settlement of dues
by Government to the lending institutions, etc.
enriched_requirements.object: of the scheme like identification of borrowers, impact assessment, implementation timelines, resolution of issues concerning

aspects of the region / State or create moral hazards in the borrower segments.

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: should also be taken into account.
